questions.q: What is the maximum allowed version hop when upgrading a Kubernetes cluster?
options: One minor version at a time (e.g. 1.28 to 1.29)
Two minor versions at a time (e.g. 1.28 to 1.30)
Any number of minor versions as long as it is within the same major version
Only patch versions can be upgraded
questions.explanation: Kubernetes only supports upgrading one minor version at a time. Upgrades spanning multiple minor versions must be performed step-by-step (e.g., 1.27 -> 1.28 -> 1.29).

questions.q: According to the Kubernetes version skew policy, which component is NOT allowed to be a higher version than the kube-apiserver?
questions.explanation: All of the above. The kube-apiserver acts as the reference version X. No other control plane components (scheduler, controller-manager) or node components (kubelet, kube-proxy) are allowed to be a higher version than the API server.

questions.q: When upgrading a worker node using kubeadm, which command performs the actual node upgrade configuration?
questions.explanation: For worker nodes, kubeadm upgrade node upgrades local configuration. kubeadm upgrade apply is only run on the first control plane node to upgrade the cluster-wide control plane components.
